parsed_review.summary: CP00-406 is a descriptor-only payments-core (RP13) closeout pack covering 10 units (RP13.P06.M05.S09-RP13.P06.M05.S18), a single micro-phase section M05 "Permission And Audit Binding" with 10 rows, following the validated CP392-CP405 pattern. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms full internal consistency: the registry binding (registry.js:239), requirements block (registry.js:1443), manifest, live closeout-pack-plan.json (pack at line 411), and contract projection (p06_permission_slice_descriptor at line 16846) all agree on pack_id CP00-406, risk_class A, unit_count 10, range RP13.P06.M05.S09-S18, micro-phase RP13.P06.M05 = 10 rows, micro-title distribution (Permission And Audit Binding:10), and deliverable distribution (implementation:2, ui:4, claude_review:1, security_audit:3 = 10, hand-verified against included_units S09-S18). The service descriptor/case-set (service.js:1846,1879) emit descriptor-only rows with safe base fields (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false) and the freezeCp406Result no-write/no-real-data attestation; the PAYMENTS_CORE_CP406_ROW_EXTRAS chain injects nothing into the 10 permission keys, so generated rows match the committed contract exactly. Validators (coverage + slice, validators.js:2587,2622) and tests (model.test.js:986,1000,1018) enforce counts, row presence, descriptor-only guards, authority boundary, no-leak guards, and contract drift, and the validator script deep-equals the contract projection against the freshly computed descriptor (validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs:828). The pack-level claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor; the Hermes packet's production_ready_candidate:true is a clearly distinct descriptor-level verification flag and emits_hermes_runtime_receipt is false, so Hermes/H13 is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence, not product runtime execution. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read_only and not final approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), human final approval required with runtime opening deferred to CP00-407, and no enterprise-trust claim from local validation (claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). Review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence/invalid_review_blockers cover all CP178+ blockers (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing fields, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims), the auth preflight passed with valid JSON (exit 0, 1434 stdout bytes), and the validation report shows only Read/Grep/Glob with forbidden_tools_present:false and no omitted active files. Changed-file scope is clean: all 7 modified files relate to CP406, index.js is unchanged because wildcard re-exports cover the new functions, the README #cp00-406 anchor and required public exports are present, and unrelated dirty/untracked files (the v2.0 docx, workbook artifacts, and the c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt belonging to a different review) are correctly excluded from the pack. Post-review artifacts (raw-output, receipt, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are appropriately absent at this pre-review stage. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
